They are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Takai District|Q11668733|WD=Q11668733|1=Takai district}} in {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Overview === *Nagano Prefecture, Suzaka City, Suzaka 1048 (Shibamiya), former kensha *Nagano Prefecture, Suzaka City, Sumisaka 1-8-1 (Hachiman), former kensha === Shibamiya === {{神社 |名称=Sumisaka Shrine (Shibamiya) |画像=[[File:Shibamiya Sumisaka-jinja Shrine ac (2).jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Suzaka|Q850461|WD=Q850461|1=Suzaka, Nagano}}Suzaka 1048 |位置={{Coord|36|39|23.8|N|138|18|40.3|E|display=inline}} |祭神=Sumisaka Ōkami |社格=former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建={{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=647|Q31111|WD=Q31111|1=647}}) |本殿= |例祭=September 14 |神事= |地図={{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|39|23.8|N|138|18|40.3|E}}}} }} According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=647|Q31111|WD=Q31111|1=647}}) by soliciting the deity from {{ill|lt=Sumisaka Shrine (Uda)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumisaka Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Uda District|Q1131315|WD=Q1131315|1=Uda District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}. In addition to the main deity, {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} is enshrined. In the Edo period, the lords of {{ill|lt=Suzaka Domain|Q585967|WD=Q585967|1=Suzaka Domain}} actively donated shrine-building sites and other support. In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine name was changed from Shibamiya to Sumisaka Shrine. The annual festival is on September 14; in addition, the Oiyama Festival, Sericulture Festival, Iyasaka Festival, and Commerce/Industry Festival are held. Shibamiya Sumisaka-jinja Shrine ac (1).jpg|Torii === Hachiman === {{神社 |名称=Sumisaka Shrine (Hachiman) |画像={{画像募集中|cat=須坂市}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Suzaka|Q850461|WD=Q850461|1=Suzaka, Nagano}}Sumisaka 1-8 |位置={{Coord|36|39|4.1|N|138|18|7.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神=Sumisaka Ōkami |社格=former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |例祭=September 22 |神事= |地図={{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|39|4.1|N|138|18|7.2|E}}}} }} Founding is unknown. In addition to the main deity, it enshrines {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinaga-tarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. A Shinto legal permit document from Keichō 2 (1597) remains for Oyama Village Sumisaka Shrine and Hachiman-gū. In the Edo period it received reverence from the lords of Suzaka Domain. In Kansei 2 (1790), the shrine name was changed from Hachiman-gū to Sumisaka Shrine. The Sumisaka Shrine framed plaque dates from Kansei 2 (1790). === Alternative theory by Toshio Hoga === Hoga argues that this shrine, together with {{ill|lt=Ikushima-Tarushima Shrine|Q11574513|WD=Q11574513|1=Ikushimatarushima Shrine}}, has the distinctive feature of appearing only in Kinai and Shinano Province, implying migration of central Kinai powerful clans that worshiped these two deities; he claims the worshiping clan was the {{ill|lt=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11595951|WD=Q11595951|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}{{ill|lt=Toshio Hoga|Q11452691|WD=Q11452691|1=Toshio Hōga}}, "2. Early stage of the Nakatomi clan - diviners and ancestors of the Nakatomi," in 'Study of Ancient Clans 5: Nakatomi Clan, descendants of ancient diviners responsible for divination,' Aogaki Publishing, 2014, p.34.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === *'Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines,' {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Co.|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun}}, 1981 *Supervised by Nagano Prefectural Shrine Association, 'Rituals of Shinshu,' Ginga Shobō, 1990 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano,' {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, 2000 {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみさかしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumisaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumisaka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:須坂市の歴史]] [[Category:須坂市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Ronsha|ja|論社|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Sumisaka Shrine (Suzaka, Suzaka City)|lt=Sumisaka Shrine (Suzaka, Suzaka City)|WD=Q135194243|1=Sumisaka Shrine (Suzaka, Suzaka City)}} ** P1352: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} ** P3831: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県須坂市須坂', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:墨坂神社 (須坂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q85878507}} == Japanese content == '''Sumisaka Shrine''' (すみさかじんじゃ) is the name of two shrines in {{ill|lt=Suzaka|Q850461|WD=Q850461|1=Suzaka, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. Both enshrine Sumisaka no Kami, and are said to have received one household of shrine allotment from the court in {{ill|lt=Ten'ō (Japan)|Q1373125|WD=Q1373125|lt=Ten'ō|1=Ten'ō}} 1 (781) (from '{{ill|lt=Shinshō Kakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}'). They are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Takai District|Q11668733|WD=Q11668733|1=Takai district}} in {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Overview === *Nagano Prefecture, Suzaka City, Suzaka 1048 (Shibamiya), former kensha *Nagano Prefecture, Suzaka City, Sumisaka 1-8-1 (Hachiman), former kensha === Shibamiya === {{神社 |名称=Sumisaka Shrine (Shibamiya) |画像=[[File:Shibamiya Sumisaka-jinja Shrine ac (2).jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Suzaka|Q850461|WD=Q850461|1=Suzaka, Nagano}}Suzaka 1048 |位置={{Coord|36|39|23.8|N|138|18|40.3|E|display=inline}} |祭神=Sumisaka Ōkami |社格=former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建={{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=647|Q31111|WD=Q31111|1=647}}) |本殿= |例祭=September 14 |神事= |地図={{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|39|23.8|N|138|18|40.3|E}}}} }} According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=647|Q31111|WD=Q31111|1=647}}) by soliciting the deity from {{ill|lt=Sumisaka Shrine (Uda)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumisaka Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Uda District|Q1131315|WD=Q1131315|1=Uda District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}. In addition to the main deity, {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} is enshrined. In the Edo period, the lords of {{ill|lt=Suzaka Domain|Q585967|WD=Q585967|1=Suzaka Domain}} actively donated shrine-building sites and other support. In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine name was changed from Shibamiya to Sumisaka Shrine. The annual festival is on September 14; in addition, the Oiyama Festival, Sericulture Festival, Iyasaka Festival, and Commerce/Industry Festival are held. Shibamiya Sumisaka-jinja Shrine ac (1).jpg|Torii === Hachiman === {{神社 |名称=Sumisaka Shrine (Hachiman) |画像={{画像募集中|cat=須坂市}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Suzaka|Q850461|WD=Q850461|1=Suzaka, Nagano}}Sumisaka 1-8 |位置={{Coord|36|39|4.1|N|138|18|7.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神=Sumisaka Ōkami |社格=former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |例祭=September 22 |神事= |地図={{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|39|4.1|N|138|18|7.2|E}}}} }} Founding is unknown. In addition to the main deity, it enshrines {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinaga-tarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. A Shinto legal permit document from Keichō 2 (1597) remains for Oyama Village Sumisaka Shrine and Hachiman-gū. In the Edo period it received reverence from the lords of Suzaka Domain. In Kansei 2 (1790), the shrine name was changed from Hachiman-gū to Sumisaka Shrine. The Sumisaka Shrine framed plaque dates from Kansei 2 (1790). === Alternative theory by Toshio Hoga === Hoga argues that this shrine, together with {{ill|lt=Ikushima-Tarushima Shrine|Q11574513|WD=Q11574513|1=Ikushimatarushima Shrine}}, has the distinctive feature of appearing only in Kinai and Shinano Province, implying migration of central Kinai powerful clans that worshiped these two deities; he claims the worshiping clan was the {{ill|lt=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11595951|WD=Q11595951|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}{{ill|lt=Toshio Hoga|Q11452691|WD=Q11452691|1=Toshio Hōga}}, "2. Early stage of the Nakatomi clan - diviners and ancestors of the Nakatomi," in 'Study of Ancient Clans 5: Nakatomi Clan, descendants of ancient diviners responsible for divination,' Aogaki Publishing, 2014, p.34.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === *'Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines,' {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Co.|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun}}, 1981 *Supervised by Nagano Prefectural Shrine Association, 'Rituals of Shinshu,' Ginga Shobō, 1990 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano,' {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, 2000 {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumisaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumisaka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]] [[Category:須坂市の歴史]] [[Category:須坂市の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

{'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} ** P3831: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県須坂市須坂', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:墨坂神社 (須坂市)]] {{wikidata link|Q85878507}} == Japanese content == '''Sumisaka Shrine''' (すみさかじんじゃ) is the name of two shrines in {{ill|lt=Suzaka|Q850461|WD=Q850461|1=Suzaka, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. Both enshrine Sumisaka no Kami, and are said to have received one household of shrine allotment from the court in {{ill|lt=Ten'ō (Japan)|Q1373125|WD=Q1373125|lt=Ten'ō|1=Ten'ō}} 1 (781) (from '{{ill|lt=Shinshō Kakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}'). They are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Takai District|Q11668733|WD=Q11668733|1=Takai district}} in {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Overview === *Nagano Prefecture, Suzaka City, Suzaka 1048 (Shibamiya), former kensha *Nagano Prefecture, Suzaka City, Sumisaka 1-8-1 (Hachiman), former kensha === Shibamiya === {{神社 |名称=Sumisaka Shrine (Shibamiya) |画像=[[File:Shibamiya Sumisaka-jinja Shrine ac (2).jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Suzaka|Q850461|WD=Q850461|1=Suzaka, Nagano}}Suzaka 1048 |位置={{Coord|36|39|23.8|N|138|18|40.3|E|display=inline}} |祭神=Sumisaka Ōkami |社格=former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建={{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=647|Q31111|WD=Q31111|1=647}}) |本殿= |例祭=September 14 |神事= |地図={{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|39|23.8|N|138|18|40.3|E}}}} }} According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=647|Q31111|WD=Q31111|1=647}}) by soliciting the deity from {{ill|lt=Sumisaka Shrine (Uda)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumisaka Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Uda District|Q1131315|WD=Q1131315|1=Uda District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}. In addition to the main deity, {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} is enshrined. In the Edo period, the lords of {{ill|lt=Suzaka Domain|Q585967|WD=Q585967|1=Suzaka Domain}} actively donated shrine-building sites and other support. In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine name was changed from Shibamiya to Sumisaka Shrine. The annual festival is on September 14; in addition, the Oiyama Festival, Sericulture Festival, Iyasaka Festival, and Commerce/Industry Festival are held. Shibamiya Sumisaka-jinja Shrine ac (1).jpg|Torii === Hachiman === {{神社 |名称=Sumisaka Shrine (Hachiman) |画像={{画像募集中|cat=須坂市}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Suzaka|Q850461|WD=Q850461|1=Suzaka, Nagano}}Sumisaka 1-8 |位置={{Coord|36|39|4.1|N|138|18|7.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神=Sumisaka Ōkami |社格=former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |例祭=September 22 |神事= |地図={{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|39|4.1|N|138|18|7.2|E}}}} }} Founding is unknown. In addition to the main deity, it enshrines {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinaga-tarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. A Shinto legal permit document from Keichō 2 (1597) remains for Oyama Village Sumisaka Shrine and Hachiman-gū. In the Edo period it received reverence from the lords of Suzaka Domain. In Kansei 2 (1790), the shrine name was changed from Hachiman-gū to Sumisaka Shrine. The Sumisaka Shrine framed plaque dates from Kansei 2 (1790). === Alternative theory by Toshio Hoga === Hoga argues that this shrine, together with {{ill|lt=Ikushima-Tarushima Shrine|Q11574513|WD=Q11574513|1=Ikushimatarushima Shrine}}, has the distinctive feature of appearing only in Kinai and Shinano Province, implying migration of central Kinai powerful clans that worshiped these two deities; he claims the worshiping clan was the {{ill|lt=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11595951|WD=Q11595951|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}{{ill|lt=Toshio Hoga|Q11452691|WD=Q11452691|1=Toshio Hōga}}, "2. Early stage of the Nakatomi clan - diviners and ancestors of the Nakatomi," in 'Study of Ancient Clans 5: Nakatomi Clan, descendants of ancient diviners responsible for divination,' Aogaki Publishing, 2014, p.34.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === *'Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines,' {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Co.|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun}}, 1981 *Supervised by Nagano Prefectural Shrine Association, 'Rituals of Shinshu,' Ginga Shobō, 1990 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano,' {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, 2000 {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumisaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumisaka Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:須坂市の歴史]] [[Category:須坂市の建築物]]

{{wikidata link|Q85878507}} == Japanese content == '''Sumisaka Shrine''' (すみさかじんじゃ) is the name of two shrines in {{ill|lt=Suzaka|Q850461|WD=Q850461|1=Suzaka, Nagano}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. Both enshrine Sumisaka no Kami, and are said to have received one household of shrine allotment from the court in {{ill|lt=Ten'ō (Japan)|Q1373125|WD=Q1373125|lt=Ten'ō|1=Ten'ō}} 1 (781) (from '{{ill|lt=Shinshō Kakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}'). They are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Takai District|Q11668733|WD=Q11668733|1=Takai district}} in {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=register of deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Overview === *Nagano Prefecture, Suzaka City, Suzaka 1048 (Shibamiya), former kensha *Nagano Prefecture, Suzaka City, Sumisaka 1-8-1 (Hachiman), former kensha === Shibamiya === {{神社 |名称=Sumisaka Shrine (Shibamiya) |画像=[[File:Shibamiya Sumisaka-jinja Shrine ac (2).jpg|250px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Suzaka|Q850461|WD=Q850461|1=Suzaka, Nagano}}Suzaka 1048 |位置={{Coord|36|39|23.8|N|138|18|40.3|E|display=inline}} |祭神=Sumisaka Ōkami |社格=former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建={{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=647|Q31111|WD=Q31111|1=647}}) |本殿= |例祭=September 14 |神事= |地図={{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|39|23.8|N|138|18|40.3|E}}}} }} According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=Hakuhō|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=647|Q31111|WD=Q31111|1=647}}) by soliciting the deity from {{ill|lt=Sumisaka Shrine (Uda)|UNKNOWN|lt=Sumisaka Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Uda District|Q1131315|WD=Q1131315|1=Uda District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}. In addition to the main deity, {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} is enshrined. In the Edo period, the lords of {{ill|lt=Suzaka Domain|Q585967|WD=Q585967|1=Suzaka Domain}} actively donated shrine-building sites and other support. In Meiji 3 (1870), the shrine name was changed from Shibamiya to Sumisaka Shrine. The annual festival is on September 14; in addition, the Oiyama Festival, Sericulture Festival, Iyasaka Festival, and Commerce/Industry Festival are held. Shibamiya Sumisaka-jinja Shrine ac (1).jpg|Torii === Hachiman === {{神社 |名称=Sumisaka Shrine (Hachiman) |画像={{画像募集中|cat=須坂市}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Suzaka|Q850461|WD=Q850461|1=Suzaka, Nagano}}Sumisaka 1-8 |位置={{Coord|36|39|4.1|N|138|18|7.2|E|display=inline}} |祭神=Sumisaka Ōkami |社格=former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建= |本殿= |例祭=September 22 |神事= |地図={{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|39|4.1|N|138|18|7.2|E}}}} }} Founding is unknown. In addition to the main deity, it enshrines {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|lt=Tarashinakatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinaga-tarashi-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. A Shinto legal permit document from Keichō 2 (1597) remains for Oyama Village Sumisaka Shrine and Hachiman-gū. In the Edo period it received reverence from the lords of Suzaka Domain. In Kansei 2 (1790), the shrine name was changed from Hachiman-gū to Sumisaka Shrine. The Sumisaka Shrine framed plaque dates from Kansei 2 (1790). === Alternative theory by Toshio Hoga === Hoga argues that this shrine, together with {{ill|lt=Ikushima-Tarushima Shrine|Q11574513|WD=Q11574513|1=Ikushimatarushima Shrine}}, has the distinctive feature of appearing only in Kinai and Shinano Province, implying migration of central Kinai powerful clans that worshiped these two deities; he claims the worshiping clan was the {{ill|lt=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11595951|WD=Q11595951|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}{{ill|lt=Toshio Hoga|Q11452691|WD=Q11452691|1=Toshio Hōga}}, "2. Early stage of the Nakatomi clan - diviners and ancestors of the Nakatomi," in 'Study of Ancient Clans 5: Nakatomi Clan, descendants of ancient diviners responsible for divination,' Aogaki Publishing, 2014, p.34.. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === *'Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines,' {{ill|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|Q11386181|WD=Q11386181|lt=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun Co.|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun}}, 1981 *Supervised by Nagano Prefectural Shrine Association, 'Rituals of Shinshu,' Ginga Shobō, 1990 *Edited by {{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, 'Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Places - 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano,' {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, 2000 {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumisaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumisaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:須坂市の歴史]] [[Category:須坂市の建築物]]
